message:
fix(codegen): Add compile-time error for PyValue callable attributes

Issue: test_binop.py stores callable `logger` as PyValue field, then tries to call it
Example: self.logger(args) where logger is runtime.PyValue

Root cause:
- Callable parameters get stored as PyValue when type is unknown
- PyValue doesn't have a call() method - calling it directly fails
- Example: class OperationLogger stores logger callable as PyValue

Fix:
- Detect when trying to call a PyValue attribute
- Emit helpful compile-time error explaining the limitation
- Points to test_binop.py as the known failing case

Known limitation: Callable attributes stored as PyValue are not yet supported
This requires proper runtime support for calling PyValue callables

Test affected: test_binop.py (OperationLogger class with logger callable)
